name: HMS Dragon

confidence_rationale: HMS Dragon has departed the UK heading to the Eastern Mediterranean. The transit route from the UK (likely Portsmouth) to the Eastern Mediterranean passes through the English Channel, Bay of Biscay, and Strait of Gibraltar. Since the description says 'departed' (suggesting early in the transit) and heading toward the Eastern Mediterranean, a reasonable mid-estimate would place the vessel in the vicinity of the Strait of Gibraltar or western Mediterranean. I've estimated coordinates near the Strait of Gibraltar (~36.0°N, ~5.0°W) as a likely position for a vessel that has departed but not yet arrived at the Eastern Mediterranean. Confidence is low due to the vagueness of the transit stage.
